Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "new_string": "def test_demonstrates_custom_cache_fragility():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates why a custom normalized-name cache would be fragile.\n\n    This test shows how a custom cache that strips or normalizes member names\n    would lose information about the actual structure of the tar archive,\n    potentially causing lookups to fail.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-cache-fragility.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Get all member names\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # Simulate what a custom cache might do: strip trailing slashes\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for name in member_names:\n                normalized_key = name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_key] = name\n\n            # Now verify that lookups still work with normalized keys\n            # (This works with Python's tarfile because it auto-normalizes,\n            # but demonstrates the concept)\n\n            # When the code needs to look up 'test/collection/plugins'\n            # it uses tar.getmember(path)\n            test_paths = [\n                'test/collection/plugins',\n                'test/collection/plugins/modules',\n            ]\n\n            for test_path in test_paths:\n                try:\n                    # This should work - looking up with exact name\n                    member = tar.getmember(test_path)\n                    assert member is not None\n                except KeyError:\n                    pytest.fail(\n                        f\"Failed to look up '{test_path}' using tar.getmember(). \"\n                        \"This shows that the current approach depends on exact \"\n                        \"string matching of path names.\"\n                    )\n\n\ndef test_extract_tar_dir_is_called_correctly():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that verifies _extract_tar_dir would be called with correct paths.\n\n    This test documents how _extract_tar_dir is used and the paths it receives.\n    The fragility occurs when directory names from the manifest don't match\n    the actual tar member names.\n    \"\"\"\n    from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir\n    from unittest import mock\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-extract.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as extract_dir:\n            with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n                # Get the directory entries from FILES.json\n                files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n                with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                    files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n                # For each directory entry in the manifest\n                for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                    if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and file_entry['name'] != '.':\n                        dir_name = file_entry['name']\n\n                        # _extract_tar_dir is called with the directory name\n                        # If the tar member doesn't exist with this exact name,\n                        # it will fail\n                        try:\n                            # This is how _extract_tar_dir looks up the member\n                            member = tar.getmember(dir_name)\n                            # Verify it's found and it's a directory\n                            assert member.isdir(), \\\n                                f\"Member '{dir_name}' should be a directory\"\n                        except KeyError:\n                            pytest.fail(\n                                f\"_extract_tar_dir would fail to extract '{dir_name}' \"\n                                \"because tar.getmember() cannot find it. \"\n                                \"This would occur if the tar archive stores directories \"\n                                \"differently than listed in FILES.json (e.g., with \"\n                                \"trailing slashes) or if a custom cache used normalized \"\n                                \"names that don't match the actual tar members.\"\n                            )\n\n\ndef test_tar_implementation_independence():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that the extraction logic is independent of tar implementation details.\n\n    The key fragility is that different tar tools might create archives with\n    slightly different member naming conventions. The code should be robust\n    to these variations.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-impl.tar.gz')\n\n        # Create a tar and then verify it can be read back correctly\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'w:gz') as tar:\n            # Add a directory\n            dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test_dir')\n            dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE\n            dir_info.mode = 0o755\n            tar.addfile(dir_info)\n\n            # Add a file in that directory\n            file_data = b'test content'\n            file_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test_dir/test_file.txt')\n            file_info.size = len(file_data)\n            tar.addfile(file_info, fileobj=__import__('io').BytesIO(file_data))\n\n        # Read it back and verify the structure is consistent\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            members = tar.getmembers()\n            member_names = [m.name for m in members]\n\n            # Verify both the directory and file are present with expected names\n            assert 'test_dir' in member_names, \"Directory should be present\"\n            assert 'test_dir/test_file.txt' in member_names, \"File should be present\"\n\n            # Verify getmember works with exact names\n            dir_member = tar.getmember('test_dir')\n            file_member = tar.getmember('test_dir/test_file.txt')\n\n            assert dir_member.isdir()\n            assert file_member.isfile()\n\n            # This shows the correct pattern: use tar.getmember() with exact names\n            # from the manifest, don't try to normalize or cache names separately",
  "old_string": "def test_demonstrates_custom_cache_fragility():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates why a custom normalized-name cache would be fragile.\n\n    This test shows how a custom cache that strips or normalizes member names\n    would lose information about the actual structure of the tar archive,\n    potentially causing lookups to fail.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-cache-fragility.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Get all member names\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # Simulate what a custom cache might do: strip trailing slashes\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for name in member_names:\n                normalized_key = name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_key] = name\n\n            # Now verify that lookups still work with normalized keys\n            # (This works with Python's tarfile because it auto-normalizes,\n            # but demonstrates the concept)\n\n            # When the code needs to look up 'test/collection/plugins'\n            # it uses tar.getmember(path)\n            test_paths = [\n                'test/collection/plugins',\n                'test/collection/plugins/modules',\n            ]\n\n            for test_path in test_paths:\n                try:\n                    # This should work - looking up with exact name\n                    member = tar.getmember(test_path)\n                    assert member is not None\n                except KeyError:\n                    pytest.fail(\n                        f\"Failed to look up '{test_path}' using tar.getmember(). \"\n                        \"This shows that the current approach depends on exact \"\n                        \"string matching of path names.\"\n                    )",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "newString": "def test_demonstrates_custom_cache_fragility():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates why a custom normalized-name cache would be fragile.\n\n    This test shows how a custom cache that strips or normalizes member names\n    would lose information about the actual structure of the tar archive,\n    potentially causing lookups to fail.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-cache-fragility.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Get all member names\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # Simulate what a custom cache might do: strip trailing slashes\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for name in member_names:\n                normalized_key = name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_key] = name\n\n            # Now verify that lookups still work with normalized keys\n            # (This works with Python's tarfile because it auto-normalizes,\n            # but demonstrates the concept)\n\n            # When the code needs to look up 'test/collection/plugins'\n            # it uses tar.getmember(path)\n            test_paths = [\n                'test/collection/plugins',\n                'test/collection/plugins/modules',\n            ]\n\n            for test_path in test_paths:\n                try:\n                    # This should work - looking up with exact name\n                    member = tar.getmember(test_path)\n                    assert member is not None\n                except KeyError:\n                    pytest.fail(\n                        f\"Failed to look up '{test_path}' using tar.getmember(). \"\n                        \"This shows that the current approach depends on exact \"\n                        \"string matching of path names.\"\n                    )\n\n\ndef test_extract_tar_dir_is_called_correctly():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that verifies _extract_tar_dir would be called with correct paths.\n\n    This test documents how _extract_tar_dir is used and the paths it receives.\n    The fragility occurs when directory names from the manifest don't match\n    the actual tar member names.\n    \"\"\"\n    from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir\n    from unittest import mock\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-extract.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as extract_dir:\n            with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n                # Get the directory entries from FILES.json\n                files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n                with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                    files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n                # For each directory entry in the manifest\n                for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                    if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and file_entry['name'] != '.':\n                        dir_name = file_entry['name']\n\n                        # _extract_tar_dir is called with the directory name\n                        # If the tar member doesn't exist with this exact name,\n                        # it will fail\n                        try:\n                            # This is how _extract_tar_dir looks up the member\n                            member = tar.getmember(dir_name)\n                            # Verify it's found and it's a directory\n                            assert member.isdir(), \\\n                                f\"Member '{dir_name}' should be a directory\"\n                        except KeyError:\n                            pytest.fail(\n                                f\"_extract_tar_dir would fail to extract '{dir_name}' \"\n                                \"because tar.getmember() cannot find it. \"\n                                \"This would occur if the tar archive stores directories \"\n                                \"differently than listed in FILES.json (e.g., with \"\n                                \"trailing slashes) or if a custom cache used normalized \"\n                                \"names that don't match the actual tar members.\"\n                            )\n\n\ndef test_tar_implementation_independence():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that the extraction logic is independent of tar implementation details.\n\n    The key fragility is that different tar tools might create archives with\n    slightly different member naming conventions. The code should be robust\n    to these variations.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-impl.tar.gz')\n\n        # Create a tar and then verify it can be read back correctly\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'w:gz') as tar:\n            # Add a directory\n            dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test_dir')\n            dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE\n            dir_info.mode = 0o755\n            tar.addfile(dir_info)\n\n            # Add a file in that directory\n            file_data = b'test content'\n            file_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test_dir/test_file.txt')\n            file_info.size = len(file_data)\n            tar.addfile(file_info, fileobj=__import__('io').BytesIO(file_data))\n\n        # Read it back and verify the structure is consistent\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            members = tar.getmembers()\n            member_names = [m.name for m in members]\n\n            # Verify both the directory and file are present with expected names\n            assert 'test_dir' in member_names, \"Directory should be present\"\n            assert 'test_dir/test_file.txt' in member_names, \"File should be present\"\n\n            # Verify getmember works with exact names\n            dir_member = tar.getmember('test_dir')\n            file_member = tar.getmember('test_dir/test_file.txt')\n\n            assert dir_member.isdir()\n            assert file_member.isfile()\n\n            # This shows the correct pattern: use tar.getmember() with exact names\n            # from the manifest, don't try to normalize or cache names separately",
  "oldString": "def test_demonstrates_custom_cache_fragility():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates why a custom normalized-name cache would be fragile.\n\n    This test shows how a custom cache that strips or normalizes member names\n    would lose information about the actual structure of the tar archive,\n    potentially causing lookups to fail.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-cache-fragility.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Get all member names\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # Simulate what a custom cache might do: strip trailing slashes\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for name in member_names:\n                normalized_key = name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_key] = name\n\n            # Now verify that lookups still work with normalized keys\n            # (This works with Python's tarfile because it auto-normalizes,\n            # but demonstrates the concept)\n\n            # When the code needs to look up 'test/collection/plugins'\n            # it uses tar.getmember(path)\n            test_paths = [\n                'test/collection/plugins',\n                'test/collection/plugins/modules',\n            ]\n\n            for test_path in test_paths:\n                try:\n                    # This should work - looking up with exact name\n                    member = tar.getmember(test_path)\n                    assert member is not None\n                except KeyError:\n                    pytest.fail(\n                        f\"Failed to look up '{test_path}' using tar.getmember(). \"\n                        \"This shows that the current approach depends on exact \"\n                        \"string matching of path names.\"\n                    )",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nRegression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.\n\nThis test demonstrates the issue where tar.getmember() lookups for directory\npaths can be fragile when the paths used in the lookup don't exactly match how\nthey're stored in the tar archive.\n\nThe fragility arises from:\n1. Relying on tar.getmember() to do exact string matching for directory names\n2. Not normalizing the paths consistently between what the manifest specifies\n   and what the tar file actually contains\n3. The potential for a custom cache to strip/normalize paths differently than\n   how the tar module stores them\n\nThis test demonstrates why using a custom normalized-name cache (that strips\ntrailing slashes or does other normalization) would be more fragile than using\nthe standard tarfile API directly with exact names.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport pytest\nimport tarfile\nimport tempfile\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\ndef create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path: str) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a minimal valid collection tar for testing.\n\n    This creates a standard collection tar with the required files.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'w:gz') as tar:\n        # Create MANIFEST.json\n        manifest = {\n            'collection_info': {\n                'namespace': 'test',\n                'name': 'collection',\n                'version': '1.0.0',\n                'authors': ['Test Author'],\n                'description': 'Test collection',\n                'license': ['GPL-3.0-or-later'],\n                'repository': None,\n                'documentation': None,\n                'homepage': None,\n                'issues': None,\n                'dependencies': {},\n            },\n            'file_manifest_file': 'FILES.json',\n            'format': 1,\n        }\n        manifest_data = json.dumps(manifest).encode('utf-8')\n        manifest_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test/collection/MANIFEST.json')\n        manifest_info.size = len(manifest_data)\n        tar.addfile(manifest_info, fileobj=__import__('io').BytesIO(manifest_data))\n\n        # Create FILES.json\n        files_content = {\n            'files': [\n                {'name': '.', 'ftype': 'dir', 'chksum_type': None, 'chksum_sha256': None},\n                {'name': 'plugins', 'ftype': 'dir', 'chksum_type': None, 'chksum_sha256': None},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules', 'ftype': 'dir', 'chksum_type': None, 'chksum_sha256': None},\n                {\n                    'name': 'plugins/modules/test.py',\n                    'ftype': 'file',\n                    'chksum_type': 'sha256',\n                    'chksum_sha256': 'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855',\n                },\n            ],\n            'format': 1,\n        }\n        files_data = json.dumps(files_content).encode('utf-8')\n        files_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test/collection/FILES.json')\n        files_info.size = len(files_data)\n        tar.addfile(files_info, fileobj=__import__('io').BytesIO(files_data))\n\n        # Add directory entries (Python's tarfile normalizes these automatically)\n        for dir_entry in ['test/collection/plugins', 'test/collection/plugins/modules']:\n            dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name=dir_entry)\n            dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE\n            dir_info.mode = 0o755\n            tar.addfile(dir_info)\n\n        # Add an empty file with matching hash\n        file_data = b''\n        file_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test/collection/plugins/modules/test.py')\n        file_info.size = len(file_data)\n        tar.addfile(file_info, fileobj=__import__('io').BytesIO(file_data))\n\n\ndef test_collection_tar_getmember_lookup_robustness():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that tar.getmember() lookups are robust for directory names.\n\n    This test demonstrates that tar.getmember() does exact string matching\n    on member names. When the manifest specifies directory names and we need\n    to look them up in the tar file, we must use the exact names stored in\n    the tar archive.\n\n    This reveals the fragility: if code used a cached/normalized version of\n    member names (stripping trailing slashes), it could fail to find members\n    when the actual tar member names differ.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-robust.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            members = tar.getmembers()\n            member_names = [m.name for m in members]\n\n            # Verify the tar has the expected structure\n            assert 'test/collection/plugins' in member_names\n            assert 'test/collection/plugins/modules' in member_names\n\n            # Test that directory lookups work with exact names\n            plugins_dir = tar.getmember('test/collection/plugins')\n            assert plugins_dir.isdir(), \"Should find plugins directory\"\n\n            modules_dir = tar.getmember('test/collection/plugins/modules')\n            assert modules_dir.isdir(), \"Should find plugins/modules directory\"\n\n\ndef test_collection_tar_member_list_completeness():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that all file entries in FILES.json exist as members in the tar.\n\n    This test verifies that when extracting a collection, all entries listed\n    in the FILES.json manifest actually exist in the tar archive with the\n    exact names specified.\n\n    This is important because if there's a mismatch (e.g., manifest says 'plugins'\n    but tar has 'plugins/'), extraction will fail.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-manifest.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Extract the FILES.json to get the expected entries\n            files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n            # Get all tar member names\n            tar_member_names = {m.name for m in tar.getmembers()}\n\n            # For each entry in the manifest (that's not '.' root)\n            for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                if file_entry['name'] == '.':\n                    continue\n\n                # Construct the full path\n                full_path = f\"test/collection/{file_entry['name']}\"\n\n                # It should exist in the tar\n                assert full_path in tar_member_names, \\\n                    f\"File entry '{file_entry['name']}' from FILES.json \" \\\n                    f\"(full path: '{full_path}') not found in tar members\"\n\n                # Verify we can look it up directly\n                try:\n                    member = tar.getmember(full_path)\n                    # Verify type matches\n                    if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir':\n                        assert member.isdir(), \\\n                            f\"'{full_path}' should be a directory\"\n                    elif file_entry['ftype'] == 'file':\n                        assert member.isfile(), \\\n                            f\"'{full_path}' should be a file\"\n                except KeyError:\n                    pytest.fail(\n                        f\"Failed to look up manifest entry '{file_entry['name']}' \"\n                        f\"(full path: '{full_path}') in tar archive. \"\n                        \"This indicates a mismatch between what FILES.json specifies \"\n                        \"and what's actually stored in the tar.\"\n                    )\n\n\ndef test_directory_path_consistency():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that directory paths in the manifest match exactly with tar member names.\n\n    This test demonstrates the potential fragility: if any part of the code\n    normalizes directory paths (strips trailing slashes, canonicalizes, etc.),\n    those normalized paths must still match the actual tar member names.\n\n    The safest approach is to use tar.getmember() directly without any\n    preprocessing of path names.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-consistency.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Get FILES.json entries\n            files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n            # Get all actual tar members\n            tar_member_dict = {m.name: m for m in tar.getmembers()}\n\n            # For each directory in FILES.json\n            for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                if file_entry['ftype'] != 'dir' or file_entry['name'] == '.':\n                    continue\n\n                # Build the expected path\n                manifest_dir_name = file_entry['name']\n                expected_full_path = f\"test/collection/{manifest_dir_name}\"\n\n                # Verify exact match in tar\n                if expected_full_path not in tar_member_dict:\n                    # Try with trailing slash (shouldn't be needed but test it)\n                    if expected_full_path + '/' in tar_member_dict:\n                        pytest.fail(\n                            f\"Directory '{manifest_dir_name}' listed in FILES.json \"\n                            f\"(expected at '{expected_full_path}') is stored in tar \"\n                            f\"with a trailing slash ('{expected_full_path}/'). \"\n                            \"This would cause tar.getmember() to fail when looking up \"\n                            \"the directory using the manifest path, demonstrating the \"\n                            \"fragility of exact string matching without accounting for \"\n                            \"trailing slash variations.\"\n                        )\n                    else:\n                        pytest.fail(\n                            f\"Directory '{manifest_dir_name}' listed in FILES.json \"\n                            f\"not found in tar at expected path '{expected_full_path}'\"\n                        )\n\n                # Verify it's actually a directory\n                member = tar_member_dict[expected_full_path]\n                assert member.isdir(), \\\n                    f\"Expected '{expected_full_path}' to be a directory\"\n\n\ndef test_demonstrates_custom_cache_fragility():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates why a custom normalized-name cache would be fragile.\n\n    This test shows how a custom cache that strips or normalizes member names\n    would lose information about the actual structure of the tar archive,\n    potentially causing lookups to fail.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-cache-fragility.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Get all member names\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # Simulate what a custom cache might do: strip trailing slashes\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for name in member_names:\n                normalized_key = name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_key] = name\n\n            # Now verify that lookups still work with normalized keys\n            # (This works with Python's tarfile because it auto-normalizes,\n            # but demonstrates the concept)\n\n            # When the code needs to look up 'test/collection/plugins'\n            # it uses tar.getmember(path)\n            test_paths = [\n                'test/collection/plugins',\n                'test/collection/plugins/modules',\n            ]\n\n            for test_path in test_paths:\n                try:\n                    # This should work - looking up with exact name\n                    member = tar.getmember(test_path)\n                    assert member is not None\n                except KeyError:\n                    pytest.fail(\n                        f\"Failed to look up '{test_path}' using tar.getmember(). \"\n                        \"This shows that the current approach depends on exact \"\n                        \"string matching of path names.\"\n                    )\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    pytest.main([__file__, '-v', '--tb=short'])\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "                     )",
        " ",
        " ",
        "+def test_extract_tar_dir_is_called_correctly():",
        "+    \"\"\"",
        "+    Test that verifies _extract_tar_dir would be called with correct paths.",
        "+",
        "+    This test documents how _extract_tar_dir is used and the paths it receives.",
        "+    The fragility occurs when directory names from the manifest don't match",
        "+    the actual tar member names.",
        "+    \"\"\"",
        "+    from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir",
        "+    from unittest import mock",
        "+",
        "+    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:",
        "+        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-extract.tar.gz')",
        "+        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)",
        "+",
        "+        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as extract_dir:",
        "+            with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:",
        "+                # Get the directory entries from FILES.json",
        "+                files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')",
        "+                with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:",
        "+                    files_json = json.loads(f.read())",
        "+",
        "+                # For each directory entry in the manifest",
        "+                for file_entry in files_json['files']:",
        "+                    if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and file_entry['name'] != '.':",
        "+                        dir_name = file_entry['name']",
        "+",
        "+                        # _extract_tar_dir is called with the directory name",
        "+                        # If the tar member doesn't exist with this exact name,",
        "+                        # it will fail",
        "+                        try:",
        "+                            # This is how _extract_tar_dir looks up the member",
        "+                            member = tar.getmember(dir_name)",
        "+                            # Verify it's found and it's a directory",
        "+                            assert member.isdir(), \\",
        "+                                f\"Member '{dir_name}' should be a directory\"",
        "+                        except KeyError:",
        "+                            pytest.fail(",
        "+                                f\"_extract_tar_dir would fail to extract '{dir_name}' \"",
        "+                                \"because tar.getmember() cannot find it. \"",
        "+                                \"This would occur if the tar archive stores directories \"",
        "+                                \"differently than listed in FILES.json (e.g., with \"",
        "+                                \"trailing slashes) or if a custom cache used normalized \"",
        "+                                \"names that don't match the actual tar members.\"",
        "+                            )",
        "+",
        "+",
        "+def test_tar_implementation_independence():",
        "+    \"\"\"",
        "+    Test that the extraction logic is independent of tar implementation details.",
        "+",
        "+    The key fragility is that different tar tools might create archives with",
        "+    slightly different member naming conventions. The code should be robust",
        "+    to these variations.",
        "+    \"\"\"",
        "+    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:",
        "+        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-impl.tar.gz')",
        "+",
        "+        # Create a tar and then verify it can be read back correctly",
        "+        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'w:gz') as tar:",
        "+            # Add a directory",
        "+            dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test_dir')",
        "+            dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE",
        "+            dir_info.mode = 0o755",
        "+            tar.addfile(dir_info)",
        "+",
        "+            # Add a file in that directory",
        "+            file_data = b'test content'",
        "+            file_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test_dir/test_file.txt')",
        "+            file_info.size = len(file_data)",
        "+            tar.addfile(file_info, fileobj=__import__('io').BytesIO(file_data))",
        "+",
        "+        # Read it back and verify the structure is consistent",
        "+        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:",
        "+            members = tar.getmembers()",
        "+            member_names = [m.name for m in members]",
        "+",
        "+            # Verify both the directory and file are present with expected names",
        "+            assert 'test_dir' in member_names, \"Directory should be present\"",
        "+            assert 'test_dir/test_file.txt' in member_names, \"File should be present\"",
        "+",
        "+            # Verify getmember works with exact names",
        "+            dir_member = tar.getmember('test_dir')",
        "+            file_member = tar.getmember('test_dir/test_file.txt')",
        "+",
        "+            assert dir_member.isdir()",
        "+            assert file_member.isfile()",
        "+",
        "+            # This shows the correct pattern: use tar.getmember() with exact names",
        "+            # from the manifest, don't try to normalize or cache names separately",
        "+",
        "+",
        " if __name__ == '__main__':",
        "     pytest.main([__file__, '-v', '--tb=short'])"
      ],
      "newLines": 97,
      "newStart": 284,
      "oldLines": 5,
      "oldStart": 284
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

37. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "new_string": "def test_extract_tar_dir_manifest_lookup():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that directory names from the manifest can be looked up in the tar.\n\n    This test documents how the collection extraction process looks up\n    directory members from the tar file. The test verifies the expected\n    behavior and documents where fragility could occur.\n\n    The issue occurs when directory names from the manifest (e.g., 'plugins')\n    don't match how they're stored in the tar (e.g., 'plugins/'). Using\n    a custom normalized-name cache would make this fragility worse.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-extract.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Get the directory entries from FILES.json\n            files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n            # For each directory entry in the manifest\n            for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and file_entry['name'] != '.':\n                    dir_name = file_entry['name']\n\n                    # When extracting, the code would be called like:\n                    # _extract_tar_dir(collection_tar, file_name, b_collection_path)\n                    # where file_name comes from the manifest\n\n                    # The first thing _extract_tar_dir does is:\n                    # tar_member = tar.getmember(dirname)\n                    # This is where fragility occurs!\n\n                    # The directory name from the manifest might be 'plugins'\n                    # but the tar might have it stored as 'plugins/'\n                    # In such a case, getmember() would fail\n\n                    try:\n                        # This simulates what _extract_tar_dir does\n                        member = tar.getmember(dir_name)\n                        # Verify it's found and it's a directory\n                        assert member.isdir(), \\\n                            f\"Member '{dir_name}' should be a directory\"\n                    except KeyError:\n                        pytest.fail(\n                            f\"tar.getmember('{dir_name}') failed with KeyError. \"\n                            \"This would cause extraction to fail. \"\n                            \"The directory name from FILES.json ('{dir_name}') \"\n                            \"doesn't match the tar member name. \"\n                            \"This could occur if the tar archive stores directories \"\n                            \"differently (e.g., with trailing slashes) or if a custom \"\n                            \"cache used normalized names that don't match actual members.\"\n                        )",
  "old_string": "def test_extract_tar_dir_is_called_correctly():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that verifies _extract_tar_dir would be called with correct paths.\n\n    This test documents how _extract_tar_dir is used and the paths it receives.\n    The fragility occurs when directory names from the manifest don't match\n    the actual tar member names.\n    \"\"\"\n    from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir\n    from unittest import mock\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-extract.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as extract_dir:\n            with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n                # Get the directory entries from FILES.json\n                files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n                with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                    files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n                # For each directory entry in the manifest\n                for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                    if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and file_entry['name'] != '.':\n                        dir_name = file_entry['name']\n\n                        # _extract_tar_dir is called with the directory name\n                        # If the tar member doesn't exist with this exact name,\n                        # it will fail\n                        try:\n                            # This is how _extract_tar_dir looks up the member\n                            member = tar.getmember(dir_name)\n                            # Verify it's found and it's a directory\n                            assert member.isdir(), \\\n                                f\"Member '{dir_name}' should be a directory\"\n                        except KeyError:\n                            pytest.fail(\n                                f\"_extract_tar_dir would fail to extract '{dir_name}' \"\n                                \"because tar.getmember() cannot find it. \"\n                                \"This would occur if the tar archive stores directories \"\n                                \"differently than listed in FILES.json (e.g., with \"\n                                \"trailing slashes) or if a custom cache used normalized \"\n                                \"names that don't match the actual tar members.\"\n                            )",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "newString": "def test_extract_tar_dir_manifest_lookup():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that directory names from the manifest can be looked up in the tar.\n\n    This test documents how the collection extraction process looks up\n    directory members from the tar file. The test verifies the expected\n    behavior and documents where fragility could occur.\n\n    The issue occurs when directory names from the manifest (e.g., 'plugins')\n    don't match how they're stored in the tar (e.g., 'plugins/'). Using\n    a custom normalized-name cache would make this fragility worse.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-extract.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Get the directory entries from FILES.json\n            files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n            # For each directory entry in the manifest\n            for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and file_entry['name'] != '.':\n                    dir_name = file_entry['name']\n\n                    # When extracting, the code would be called like:\n                    # _extract_tar_dir(collection_tar, file_name, b_collection_path)\n                    # where file_name comes from the manifest\n\n                    # The first thing _extract_tar_dir does is:\n                    # tar_member = tar.getmember(dirname)\n                    # This is where fragility occurs!\n\n                    # The directory name from the manifest might be 'plugins'\n                    # but the tar might have it stored as 'plugins/'\n                    # In such a case, getmember() would fail\n\n                    try:\n                        # This simulates what _extract_tar_dir does\n                        member = tar.getmember(dir_name)\n                        # Verify it's found and it's a directory\n                        assert member.isdir(), \\\n                            f\"Member '{dir_name}' should be a directory\"\n                    except KeyError:\n                        pytest.fail(\n                            f\"tar.getmember('{dir_name}') failed with KeyError. \"\n                            \"This would cause extraction to fail. \"\n                            \"The directory name from FILES.json ('{dir_name}') \"\n                            \"doesn't match the tar member name. \"\n                            \"This could occur if the tar archive stores directories \"\n                            \"differently (e.g., with trailing slashes) or if a custom \"\n                            \"cache used normalized names that don't match actual members.\"\n                        )",
  "oldString": "def test_extract_tar_dir_is_called_correctly():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that verifies _extract_tar_dir would be called with correct paths.\n\n    This test documents how _extract_tar_dir is used and the paths it receives.\n    The fragility occurs when directory names from the manifest don't match\n    the actual tar member names.\n    \"\"\"\n    from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir\n    from unittest import mock\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-extract.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as extract_dir:\n            with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n                # Get the directory entries from FILES.json\n                files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n                with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                    files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n                # For each directory entry in the manifest\n                for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                    if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and file_entry['name'] != '.':\n                        dir_name = file_entry['name']\n\n                        # _extract_tar_dir is called with the directory name\n                        # If the tar member doesn't exist with this exact name,\n                        # it will fail\n                        try:\n                            # This is how _extract_tar_dir looks up the member\n                            member = tar.getmember(dir_name)\n                            # Verify it's found and it's a directory\n                            assert member.isdir(), \\\n                                f\"Member '{dir_name}' should be a directory\"\n                        except KeyError:\n                            pytest.fail(\n                                f\"_extract_tar_dir would fail to extract '{dir_name}' \"\n                                \"because tar.getmember() cannot find it. \"\n                                \"This would occur if the tar archive stores directories \"\n                                \"differently than listed in FILES.json (e.g., with \"\n                                \"trailing slashes) or if a custom cache used normalized \"\n                                \"names that don't match the actual tar members.\"\n                            )",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nRegression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.\n\nThis test demonstrates the issue where tar.getmember() lookups for directory\npaths can be fragile when the paths used in the lookup don't exactly match how\nthey're stored in the tar archive.\n\nThe fragility arises from:\n1. Relying on tar.getmember() to do exact string matching for directory names\n2. Not normalizing the paths consistently between what the manifest specifies\n   and what the tar file actually contains\n3. The potential for a custom cache to strip/normalize paths differently than\n   how the tar module stores them\n\nThis test demonstrates why using a custom normalized-name cache (that strips\ntrailing slashes or does other normalization) would be more fragile than using\nthe standard tarfile API directly with exact names.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport pytest\nimport tarfile\nimport tempfile\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\ndef create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path: str) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a minimal valid collection tar for testing.\n\n    This creates a standard collection tar with the required files.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'w:gz') as tar:\n        # Create MANIFEST.json\n        manifest = {\n            'collection_info': {\n                'namespace': 'test',\n                'name': 'collection',\n                'version': '1.0.0',\n                'authors': ['Test Author'],\n                'description': 'Test collection',\n                'license': ['GPL-3.0-or-later'],\n                'repository': None,\n                'documentation': None,\n                'homepage': None,\n                'issues': None,\n                'dependencies': {},\n            },\n            'file_manifest_file': 'FILES.json',\n            'format': 1,\n        }\n        manifest_data = json.dumps(manifest).encode('utf-8')\n        manifest_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test/collection/MANIFEST.json')\n        manifest_info.size = len(manifest_data)\n        tar.addfile(manifest_info, fileobj=__import__('io').BytesIO(manifest_data))\n\n        # Create FILES.json\n        files_content = {\n            'files': [\n                {'name': '.', 'ftype': 'dir', 'chksum_type': None, 'chksum_sha256': None},\n                {'name': 'plugins', 'ftype': 'dir', 'chksum_type': None, 'chksum_sha256': None},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules', 'ftype': 'dir', 'chksum_type': None, 'chksum_sha256': None},\n                {\n                    'name': 'plugins/modules/test.py',\n                    'ftype': 'file',\n                    'chksum_type': 'sha256',\n                    'chksum_sha256': 'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855',\n                },\n            ],\n            'format': 1,\n        }\n        files_data = json.dumps(files_content).encode('utf-8')\n        files_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test/collection/FILES.json')\n        files_info.size = len(files_data)\n        tar.addfile(files_info, fileobj=__import__('io').BytesIO(files_data))\n\n        # Add directory entries (Python's tarfile normalizes these automatically)\n        for dir_entry in ['test/collection/plugins', 'test/collection/plugins/modules']:\n            dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name=dir_entry)\n            dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE\n            dir_info.mode = 0o755\n            tar.addfile(dir_info)\n\n        # Add an empty file with matching hash\n        file_data = b''\n        file_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test/collection/plugins/modules/test.py')\n        file_info.size = len(file_data)\n        tar.addfile(file_info, fileobj=__import__('io').BytesIO(file_data))\n\n\ndef test_collection_tar_getmember_lookup_robustness():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that tar.getmember() lookups are robust for directory names.\n\n    This test demonstrates that tar.getmember() does exact string matching\n    on member names. When the manifest specifies directory names and we need\n    to look them up in the tar file, we must use the exact names stored in\n    the tar archive.\n\n    This reveals the fragility: if code used a cached/normalized version of\n    member names (stripping trailing slashes), it could fail to find members\n    when the actual tar member names differ.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-robust.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            members = tar.getmembers()\n            member_names = [m.name for m in members]\n\n            # Verify the tar has the expected structure\n            assert 'test/collection/plugins' in member_names\n            assert 'test/collection/plugins/modules' in member_names\n\n            # Test that directory lookups work with exact names\n            plugins_dir = tar.getmember('test/collection/plugins')\n            assert plugins_dir.isdir(), \"Should find plugins directory\"\n\n            modules_dir = tar.getmember('test/collection/plugins/modules')\n            assert modules_dir.isdir(), \"Should find plugins/modules directory\"\n\n\ndef test_collection_tar_member_list_completeness():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that all file entries in FILES.json exist as members in the tar.\n\n    This test verifies that when extracting a collection, all entries listed\n    in the FILES.json manifest actually exist in the tar archive with the\n    exact names specified.\n\n    This is important because if there's a mismatch (e.g., manifest says 'plugins'\n    but tar has 'plugins/'), extraction will fail.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-manifest.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Extract the FILES.json to get the expected entries\n            files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n            # Get all tar member names\n            tar_member_names = {m.name for m in tar.getmembers()}\n\n            # For each entry in the manifest (that's not '.' root)\n            for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                if file_entry['name'] == '.':\n                    continue\n\n                # Construct the full path\n                full_path = f\"test/collection/{file_entry['name']}\"\n\n                # It should exist in the tar\n                assert full_path in tar_member_names, \\\n                    f\"File entry '{file_entry['name']}' from FILES.json \" \\\n                    f\"(full path: '{full_path}') not found in tar members\"\n\n                # Verify we can look it up directly\n                try:\n                    member = tar.getmember(full_path)\n                    # Verify type matches\n                    if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir':\n                        assert member.isdir(), \\\n                            f\"'{full_path}' should be a directory\"\n                    elif file_entry['ftype'] == 'file':\n                        assert member.isfile(), \\\n                            f\"'{full_path}' should be a file\"\n                except KeyError:\n                    pytest.fail(\n                        f\"Failed to look up manifest entry '{file_entry['name']}' \"\n                        f\"(full path: '{full_path}') in tar archive. \"\n                        \"This indicates a mismatch between what FILES.json specifies \"\n                        \"and what's actually stored in the tar.\"\n                    )\n\n\ndef test_directory_path_consistency():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that directory paths in the manifest match exactly with tar member names.\n\n    This test demonstrates the potential fragility: if any part of the code\n    normalizes directory paths (strips trailing slashes, canonicalizes, etc.),\n    those normalized paths must still match the actual tar member names.\n\n    The safest approach is to use tar.getmember() directly without any\n    preprocessing of path names.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-consistency.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Get FILES.json entries\n            files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n            # Get all actual tar members\n            tar_member_dict = {m.name: m for m in tar.getmembers()}\n\n            # For each directory in FILES.json\n            for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                if file_entry['ftype'] != 'dir' or file_entry['name'] == '.':\n                    continue\n\n                # Build the expected path\n                manifest_dir_name = file_entry['name']\n                expected_full_path = f\"test/collection/{manifest_dir_name}\"\n\n                # Verify exact match in tar\n                if expected_full_path not in tar_member_dict:\n                    # Try with trailing slash (shouldn't be needed but test it)\n                    if expected_full_path + '/' in tar_member_dict:\n                        pytest.fail(\n                            f\"Directory '{manifest_dir_name}' listed in FILES.json \"\n                            f\"(expected at '{expected_full_path}') is stored in tar \"\n                            f\"with a trailing slash ('{expected_full_path}/'). \"\n                            \"This would cause tar.getmember() to fail when looking up \"\n                            \"the directory using the manifest path, demonstrating the \"\n                            \"fragility of exact string matching without accounting for \"\n                            \"trailing slash variations.\"\n                        )\n                    else:\n                        pytest.fail(\n                            f\"Directory '{manifest_dir_name}' listed in FILES.json \"\n                            f\"not found in tar at expected path '{expected_full_path}'\"\n                        )\n\n                # Verify it's actually a directory\n                member = tar_member_dict[expected_full_path]\n                assert member.isdir(), \\\n                    f\"Expected '{expected_full_path}' to be a directory\"\n\n\ndef test_demonstrates_custom_cache_fragility():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates why a custom normalized-name cache would be fragile.\n\n    This test shows how a custom cache that strips or normalizes member names\n    would lose information about the actual structure of the tar archive,\n    potentially causing lookups to fail.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-cache-fragility.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            # Get all member names\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # Simulate what a custom cache might do: strip trailing slashes\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for name in member_names:\n                normalized_key = name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_key] = name\n\n            # Now verify that lookups still work with normalized keys\n            # (This works with Python's tarfile because it auto-normalizes,\n            # but demonstrates the concept)\n\n            # When the code needs to look up 'test/collection/plugins'\n            # it uses tar.getmember(path)\n            test_paths = [\n                'test/collection/plugins',\n                'test/collection/plugins/modules',\n            ]\n\n            for test_path in test_paths:\n                try:\n                    # This should work - looking up with exact name\n                    member = tar.getmember(test_path)\n                    assert member is not None\n                except KeyError:\n                    pytest.fail(\n                        f\"Failed to look up '{test_path}' using tar.getmember(). \"\n                        \"This shows that the current approach depends on exact \"\n                        \"string matching of path names.\"\n                    )\n\n\ndef test_extract_tar_dir_is_called_correctly():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that verifies _extract_tar_dir would be called with correct paths.\n\n    This test documents how _extract_tar_dir is used and the paths it receives.\n    The fragility occurs when directory names from the manifest don't match\n    the actual tar member names.\n    \"\"\"\n    from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir\n    from unittest import mock\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-extract.tar.gz')\n        create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)\n\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as extract_dir:\n            with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n                # Get the directory entries from FILES.json\n                files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')\n                with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                    files_json = json.loads(f.read())\n\n                # For each directory entry in the manifest\n                for file_entry in files_json['files']:\n                    if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and file_entry['name'] != '.':\n                        dir_name = file_entry['name']\n\n                        # _extract_tar_dir is called with the directory name\n                        # If the tar member doesn't exist with this exact name,\n                        # it will fail\n                        try:\n                            # This is how _extract_tar_dir looks up the member\n                            member = tar.getmember(dir_name)\n                            # Verify it's found and it's a directory\n                            assert member.isdir(), \\\n                                f\"Member '{dir_name}' should be a directory\"\n                        except KeyError:\n                            pytest.fail(\n                                f\"_extract_tar_dir would fail to extract '{dir_name}' \"\n                                \"because tar.getmember() cannot find it. \"\n                                \"This would occur if the tar archive stores directories \"\n                                \"differently than listed in FILES.json (e.g., with \"\n                                \"trailing slashes) or if a custom cache used normalized \"\n                                \"names that don't match the actual tar members.\"\n                            )\n\n\ndef test_tar_implementation_independence():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that the extraction logic is independent of tar implementation details.\n\n    The key fragility is that different tar tools might create archives with\n    slightly different member naming conventions. The code should be robust\n    to these variations.\n    \"\"\"\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-impl.tar.gz')\n\n        # Create a tar and then verify it can be read back correctly\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'w:gz') as tar:\n            # Add a directory\n            dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test_dir')\n            dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE\n            dir_info.mode = 0o755\n            tar.addfile(dir_info)\n\n            # Add a file in that directory\n            file_data = b'test content'\n            file_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='test_dir/test_file.txt')\n            file_info.size = len(file_data)\n            tar.addfile(file_info, fileobj=__import__('io').BytesIO(file_data))\n\n        # Read it back and verify the structure is consistent\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:\n            members = tar.getmembers()\n            member_names = [m.name for m in members]\n\n            # Verify both the directory and file are present with expected names\n            assert 'test_dir' in member_names, \"Directory should be present\"\n            assert 'test_dir/test_file.txt' in member_names, \"File should be present\"\n\n            # Verify getmember works with exact names\n            dir_member = tar.getmember('test_dir')\n            file_member = tar.getmember('test_dir/test_file.txt')\n\n            assert dir_member.isdir()\n            assert file_member.isfile()\n\n            # This shows the correct pattern: use tar.getmember() with exact names\n            # from the manifest, don't try to normalize or cache names separately\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    pytest.main([__file__, '-v', '--tb=short'])\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "                     )",
        " ",
        " ",
        "-def test_extract_tar_dir_is_called_correctly():",
        "+def test_extract_tar_dir_manifest_lookup():",
        "     \"\"\"",
        "-    Test that verifies _extract_tar_dir would be called with correct paths.",
        "+    Test that directory names from the manifest can be looked up in the tar.",
        " ",
        "-    This test documents how _extract_tar_dir is used and the paths it receives.",
        "-    The fragility occurs when directory names from the manifest don't match",
        "-    the actual tar member names.",
        "-    \"\"\"",
        "-    from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir",
        "-    from unittest import mock",
        "+    This test documents how the collection extraction process looks up",
        "+    directory members from the tar file. The test verifies the expected",
        "+    behavior and documents where fragility could occur.",
        " ",
        "+    The issue occurs when directory names from the manifest (e.g., 'plugins')",
        "+    don't match how they're stored in the tar (e.g., 'plugins/'). Using",
        "+    a custom normalized-name cache would make this fragility worse.",
        "+    \"\"\"",
        "     with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:",
        "         tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test-extract.tar.gz')",
        "         create_minimal_collection_tar(tar_path)",
        " ",
        "-        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as extract_dir:",
        "-            with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:",
        "-                # Get the directory entries from FILES.json",
        "-                files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')",
        "-                with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:",
        "-                    files_json = json.loads(f.read())",
        "+        with tarfile.open(tar_path, 'r:gz') as tar:",
        "+            # Get the directory entries from FILES.json",
        "+            files_member = tar.getmember('test/collection/FILES.json')",
        "+            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:",
        "+                files_json = json.loads(f.read())",
        " ",
        "-                # For each directory entry in the manifest",
        "-                for file_entry in files_json['files']:",
        "-                    if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and file_entry['name'] != '.':",
        "-                        dir_name = file_entry['name']",
        "+            # For each directory entry in the manifest",
        "+            for file_entry in files_json['files']:",
        "+                if file_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and file_entry['name'] != '.':",
        "+                    dir_name = file_entry['name']",
        " ",
        "-                        # _extract_tar_dir is called with the directory name",
        "-                        # If the tar member doesn't exist with this exact name,",
        "-                        # it will fail",
        "-                        try:",
        "-                            # This is how _extract_tar_dir looks up the member",
        "-                            member = tar.getmember(dir_name)",
        "-                            # Verify it's found and it's a directory",
        "-                            assert member.isdir(), \\",
        "-                                f\"Member '{dir_name}' should be a directory\"",
        "-                        except KeyError:",
        "-                            pytest.fail(",
        "-                                f\"_extract_tar_dir would fail to extract '{dir_name}' \"",
        "-                                \"because tar.getmember() cannot find it. \"",
        "-                                \"This would occur if the tar archive stores directories \"",
        "-                                \"differently than listed in FILES.json (e.g., with \"",
        "-                                \"trailing slashes) or if a custom cache used normalized \"",
        "-                                \"names that don't match the actual tar members.\"",
        "-                            )",
        "+                    # When extracting, the code would be called like:",
        "+                    # _extract_tar_dir(collection_tar, file_name, b_collection_path)",
        "+                    # where file_name comes from the manifest",
        " ",
        "+                    # The first thing _extract_tar_dir does is:",
        "+                    # tar_member = tar.getmember(dirname)",
        "+                    # This is where fragility occurs!",
        " ",
        "+                    # The directory name from the manifest might be 'plugins'",
        "+                    # but the tar might have it stored as 'plugins/'",
        "+                    # In such a case, getmember() would fail",
        "+",
        "+                    try:",
        "+                        # This simulates what _extract_tar_dir does",
        "+                        member = tar.getmember(dir_name)",
        "+                        # Verify it's found and it's a directory",
        "+                        assert member.isdir(), \\",
        "+                            f\"Member '{dir_name}' should be a directory\"",
        "+                    except KeyError:",
        "+                        pytest.fail(",
        "+                            f\"tar.getmember('{dir_name}') failed with KeyError. \"",
        "+                            \"This would cause extraction to fail. \"",
        "+                            \"The directory name from FILES.json ('{dir_name}') \"",
        "+                            \"doesn't match the tar member name. \"",
        "+                            \"This could occur if the tar archive stores directories \"",
        "+                            \"differently (e.g., with trailing slashes) or if a custom \"",
        "+                            \"cache used normalized names that don't match actual members.\"",
        "+                        )",
        "+",
        "+",
        " def test_tar_implementation_independence():",
        "     \"\"\"",
        "     Test that the extraction logic is independent of tar implementation details."
      ],
      "newLines": 63,
      "newStart": 284,
      "oldLines": 53,
      "oldStart": 284
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1195: def _build_files_manifest_walk(b_collection_path, namespace, name, ignore_patterns):\\n1196:     # type: (bytes, str, str, list[str]) -> FilesManifestType\\n1197:     # We always ignore .pyc and .retry files as well as some well known version control directories. The ignore\\n1198:     # patterns can be extended by the build_ignore key in galaxy.yml\\n1199:     b_ignore_patterns = [\\n1200:         b'MANIFEST.json',\\n1201:         b'FILES.json',\\n1202:         b'galaxy.yml',\\n1203:         b'galaxy.yaml',\\n1204:         b'.git',\\n1205:         b'*.pyc',\\n1206:         b'*.retry',\\n1207:         b'tests/output',  # Ignore ansible-test result output directory.\\n1208:         to_bytes('{0}-{1}-*.tar.gz'.format(namespace, name)),  # Ignores previously built artifacts in the root dir.\\n1209:     ]\\n1210:     b_ignore_patterns += [to_bytes(p) for p in ignore_patterns]\\n1211:     b_ignore_dirs = frozenset([b'CVS', b'.bzr', b'.hg', b'.git', b'.svn', b'__pycache__', b'.tox'])\\n1212: \\n1213:     manifest = _make_manifest()\\n1214: \\n1215:     def _discover_relative_base_directory(b_path: bytes, b_top_level_dir: bytes) -> bytes:\\n1216:         if b_path == b_top_level_dir:\\n1217:             return b''\\n1218:         common_prefix = os.path.commonpath((b_top_level_dir, b_path))\\n1219:         b_rel_base_dir = os.path.relpath(b_path, common_prefix)\\n1220:         return b_rel_base_dir.lstrip(os.path.sep.encode())\\n1221: \\n1222:     def _walk(b_path, b_top_level_dir):\\n1223:         b_rel_base_dir = _discover_relative_base_directory(b_path, b_top_level_dir)\\n1224:         for b_item in os.listdir(b_path):\\n1225:             b_abs_path = os.path.join(b_path, b_item)\\n1226:             b_rel_path = os.path.join(b_rel_base_dir, b_item)\\n1227:             rel_path = to_text(b_rel_path, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\\n1228: \\n1229:             if os.path.isdir(b_abs_path):\\n1230:                 if any(b_item == b_path for b_path in b_ignore_dirs) or \\\\\\n1231:                         any(fnmatch.fnmatch(b_rel_path, b_pattern) for b_pattern in b_ignore_patterns):\\n1232:                     display.vvv(\\\"Skipping '%s' for collection build\\\" % to_text(b_abs_path))\\n1233:                     continue\\n1234: \\n1235:                 if os.path.islink(b_abs_path):\\n1236:                     b_link_target = os.path.realpath(b_abs_path)\\n1237: \\n1238:                     if not _is_child_path(b_link_target, b_top_level_dir):\\n1239:                         display.warning(\\\"Skipping '%s' as it is a symbolic link to a directory outside the collection\\\"\\n1240:                                         % to_text(b_abs_path))\\n1241:                         continue\\n1242: \\n1243:                 manifest['files'].append(_make_entry(rel_path, 'dir'))\\n1244: \\n1245:                 if not os.path.islink(b_abs_path):\\n1246:                     _walk(b_abs_path, b_top_level_dir)\\n1247:             else:\\n1248:                 if any(fnmatch.fnmatch(b_rel_path, b_pattern) for b_pattern in b_ignore_patterns):\\n1249:                     display.vvv(\\\"Skipping '%s' for collection build\\\" % to_text(b_abs_path))\\n1250:                     continue\\n1251: \\n1252:                 # Handling of file symlinks occur in _build_collection_tar, the manifest for a symlink is the same for\\n1253:                 # a normal file.\\n1254:                 manifest['files'].append(\\n1255:                     _make_entry(\\n1256:                         rel_path,\\n1257:                         'file',\\n1258:                         chksum_type='sha256',\\n1259:                         chksum=secure_hash(b_abs_path, hash_func=sha256)\\n1260:                     )\\n1261:                 )\\n1262: \\n1263:     _walk(b_collection_path, b_collection_path)\\n1264: \\n1265:     return manifest\\n1266: \\n1267: \\n1268: # FIXME: accept a dict produced from `galaxy.yml` instead of separate args\\n1269: def _build_manifest(namespace, name, version, authors, readme, tags, description, license_file,\\n1270:                     dependencies, repository, documentation, homepage, issues, **kwargs):\\n1271:     manifest = {\\n1272:         'collection_info': {\\n1273:             'namespace': namespace,\\n1274:             'name': name,\\n1275:             'version': version,\\n1276:             'authors': authors,\\n1277:             'readme': readme,\\n1278:             'tags': tags,\\n1279:             'description': description,\\n1280:             'license': kwargs['license'],\\n1281: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ansible--b2a289dc@b2a289dcbb702003377221e25f62c8a3608f0e89/-/blob/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py%3FL1195-1280\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1195: def _build_files_manifest_walk(b_collection_path, namespace, name, ignore_patterns):\n1196:     # type: (bytes, str, str, list[str]) -> FilesManifestType\n1197:     # We always ignore .pyc and .retry files as well as some well known version control directories. The ignore\n1198:     # patterns can be extended by the build_ignore key in galaxy.yml\n1199:     b_ignore_patterns = [\n1200:         b'MANIFEST.json',\n1201:         b'FILES.json',\n1202:         b'galaxy.yml',\n1203:         b'galaxy.yaml',\n1204:         b'.git',\n1205:         b'*.pyc',\n1206:         b'*.retry',\n1207:         b'tests/output',  # Ignore ansible-test result output directory.\n1208:         to_bytes('{0}-{1}-*.tar.gz'.format(namespace, name)),  # Ignores previously built artifacts in the root dir.\n1209:     ]\n1210:     b_ignore_patterns += [to_bytes(p) for p in ignore_patterns]\n1211:     b_ignore_dirs = frozenset([b'CVS', b'.bzr', b'.hg', b'.git', b'.svn', b'__pycache__', b'.tox'])\n1212: \n1213:     manifest = _make_manifest()\n1214: \n1215:     def _discover_relative_base_directory(b_path: bytes, b_top_level_dir: bytes) -> bytes:\n1216:         if b_path == b_top_level_dir:\n1217:             return b''\n1218:         common_prefix = os.path.commonpath((b_top_level_dir, b_path))\n1219:         b_rel_base_dir = os.path.relpath(b_path, common_prefix)\n1220:         return b_rel_base_dir.lstrip(os.path.sep.encode())\n1221: \n1222:     def _walk(b_path, b_top_level_dir):\n1223:         b_rel_base_dir = _discover_relative_base_directory(b_path, b_top_level_dir)\n1224:         for b_item in os.listdir(b_path):\n1225:             b_abs_path = os.path.join(b_path, b_item)\n1226:             b_rel_path = os.path.join(b_rel_base_dir, b_item)\n1227:             rel_path = to_text(b_rel_path, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n1228: \n1229:             if os.path.isdir(b_abs_path):\n1230:                 if any(b_item == b_path for b_path in b_ignore_dirs) or \\\n1231:                         any(fnmatch.fnmatch(b_rel_path, b_pattern) for b_pattern in b_ignore_patterns):\n1232:                     display.vvv(\"Skipping '%s' for collection build\" % to_text(b_abs_path))\n1233:                     continue\n1234: \n1235:                 if os.path.islink(b_abs_path):\n1236:                     b_link_target = os.path.realpath(b_abs_path)\n1237: \n1238:                     if not _is_child_path(b_link_target, b_top_level_dir):\n1239:                         display.warning(\"Skipping '%s' as it is a symbolic link to a directory outside the collection\"\n1240:                                         % to_text(b_abs_path))\n1241:                         continue\n1242: \n1243:                 manifest['files'].append(_make_entry(rel_path, 'dir'))\n1244: \n1245:                 if not os.path.islink(b_abs_path):\n1246:                     _walk(b_abs_path, b_top_level_dir)\n1247:             else:\n1248:                 if any(fnmatch.fnmatch(b_rel_path, b_pattern) for b_pattern in b_ignore_patterns):\n1249:                     display.vvv(\"Skipping '%s' for collection build\" % to_text(b_abs_path))\n1250:                     continue\n1251: \n1252:                 # Handling of file symlinks occur in _build_collection_tar, the manifest for a symlink is the same for\n1253:                 # a normal file.\n1254:                 manifest['files'].append(\n1255:                     _make_entry(\n1256:                         rel_path,\n1257:                         'file',\n1258:                         chksum_type='sha256',\n1259:                         chksum=secure_hash(b_abs_path, hash_func=sha256)\n1260:                     )\n1261:                 )\n1262: \n1263:     _walk(b_collection_path, b_collection_path)\n1264: \n1265:     return manifest\n1266: \n1267: \n1268: # FIXME: accept a dict produced from `galaxy.yml` instead of separate args\n1269: def _build_manifest(namespace, name, version, authors, readme, tags, description, license_file,\n1270:                     dependencies, repository, documentation, homepage, issues, **kwargs):\n1271:     manifest = {\n1272:         'collection_info': {\n1273:             'namespace': namespace,\n1274:             'name': name,\n1275:             'version': version,\n1276:             'authors': authors,\n1277:             'readme': readme,\n1278:             'tags': tags,\n1279:             'description': description,\n1280:             'license': kwargs['license'],\n1281: ",

  }
}
